justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified clearly in integration assessment and as must-have #1, (2) API call duration gap identified in observability review and as must-have #3, (3) Metric label (valid/invalid) gap identified in metrics gaps and as must-have #2, (4) Latency histogram absence noted explicitly with recommended buckets, (5) PII consideration flagged appropriately — item #9 recommends logging address 'hashed or truncated for PII' rather than blindly logging raw input, treating it as a decision point, (6) Verdict is NOT READY, (7) Recommendations are highly specific and actionable with concrete examples.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough output — goes well beyond the rubric requirements with resilience assessment, health check coupling analysis, and escalation routing
Request ID gap identified in multiple places (integration, observability, recommendations) with clear explanation of why it matters
Metric label gap called out precisely: 'validation_requests_total has no valid/invalid label — cannot track validation success rate'
Latency histogram recommendation includes specific bucket values (0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10)
PII handled correctly — recommends 'hashed or truncated for PII' rather than logging raw addresses, positioned as a should-have not a must-have
The '3am scenario' narrative is an effective way to communicate the debugging gap to stakeholders
Recommendations are prioritized into must-have/should-have/nice-to-have tiers with clear rationale

Minor: PII flagging could be slightly more explicit as a 'decision point requiring team input' rather than just noting the mitigation technique — but the placement in should-haves (not must-haves) implicitly treats it as a judgment call
